controls.contextLossWarning: Sorry, a graphics error occurred. Reload the page to continue.

screenSummary.playArea: A three-dimensional view of an electromagnetic wave traveling along a horizontal axis, shown as an animated curve of its electric field with field arrows at both ends.
screenSummary.controlArea: Controls set the wave's polarization, amplitude, and wavelength. View buttons change the camera angle, a time control plays, pauses, or steps the animation, and a Reset All button restores the starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Change the wave's polarization, amplitude, or wavelength, or rotate the 3D view.

intro.currentDetailsPattern: A {{polarization}} wave with amplitude {{amplitude}} of 10 and wavelength number {{wavelength}} of 8 is {{motionState}}.

waveView.accessibleHelpText: Rotate the view with the arrow keys; zoom with plus and minus.

screenSummary.playArea: A three-dimensional view of two electromagnetic waves traveling along a shared horizontal axis, shown as animated curves of their electric fields, plus an optional superposition curve and an optional polarizing filter centered on the axis.
screenSummary.controlArea: Controls set each wave's polarization and amplitude, the shared wavelength, and the phase difference between the waves. A polarizer section inserts a dichroic filter and sets how strongly it absorbs each wave. View buttons change the camera angle, a time control plays, pauses, or steps the animation, and a Reset All button restores the starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Change a wave's polarization or amplitude, show the sum of the two waves, or insert the polarizer.

polarization.currentDetailsPattern: Wave 1 is {{polarization1}} with amplitude {{amplitude1}} of 10, and wave 2 is {{polarization2}} with amplitude {{amplitude2}} of 10; the phase difference is {{phase}} degrees. The polarizer is {{polarizerState}}. The animation is {{motionState}}.

waveView.accessibleHelpText: Rotate the view with the arrow keys; zoom with plus and minus.

screenSummary.playArea: A three-dimensional view of the vertical and horizontal components of a light wave traveling along a horizontal axis, their superposition, and a birefringent wave plate centered on the axis that delays one component relative to the other.
screenSummary.controlArea: Controls set each component's amplitude, the phase difference, and the wave plate's length and per-component refractive indices. Quarter-wave and half-wave buttons configure the plate exactly, and a readout reports the resulting retardation. View buttons change the camera angle, a time control plays, pauses, or steps the animation, and a Reset All button restores the starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Press the quarter-wave or half-wave plate button, or adjust the refractive indices, and watch the polarization of the outgoing sum change.

wavePlates.currentDetailsPattern: The vertical component has amplitude {{amplitude1}} of 10 and the horizontal component has amplitude {{amplitude2}} of 10; the phase difference is {{phase}} degrees. The wave plate is {{plateState}} with a retardation of {{retardation}} degrees. The animation is {{motionState}}.

waveView.accessibleHelpText: Rotate the view with the arrow keys; zoom with plus and minus.

screenSummary.playArea: A three-dimensional view of up to two electromagnetic waves traveling along a horizontal axis, their superposition, and an optional material slab whose refractive index and extinction coefficient for each wave produce absorption, refraction, dichroism, birefringence, and optical activity.
screenSummary.controlArea: A preset menu offers twenty ready-made phenomena. Controls set each wave's polarization, amplitude, wavelength, phase, and direction, and the material's length and per-wave optical constants. Display checkboxes toggle the curves, field vectors, and magnetic field. View buttons change the camera angle, a time control plays, pauses, or steps the animation, and a Reset All button restores the starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Pick a preset from the Show me menu, or build your own configuration with the wave and material controls.

lab.currentDetailsPattern: Wave 1 is {{wave1State}}, and wave 2 is {{wave2State}}. The material is {{materialState}}. The animation is {{motionState}}.

waveView.accessibleHelpText: Rotate the view with the arrow keys; zoom with plus and minus.
